Market Intelligence Brief
Generated 12:31 ET
P/C divergence signals institutional dispersion hedge as NFP looms
KEY TAKEAWAYS
  • 1. Index puts at 1.67 vs single-name calls at 0.90 reveals classic dispersion hedge positioning into Friday's NFP.
  • 2. Negative $13B SPY gamma amplifies directional moves while low 0.25 implied correlation favors stock-picking.
  • 3. Energy leads with XLE overbought while mega-cap tech oversold creates tactical rotation opportunity.
NARRATIVE — dispersion hedge pre-NFP

The most striking signal today is the extreme P/C divergence — index puts heavy at 1.67 while single-name calls dominate at 0.90, a classic institutional dispersion hedge ahead of Friday's NFP. This positioning makes sense given implied correlation at just 0.25, creating optimal conditions for stock-specific outperformance while hedging broad market risk. The massive SPY put flow (378K Mar 31 640P, 318K 639P) confirms systematic downside protection is being layered in before employment data. Dealer positioning amplifies this setup, with SPY's negative $13B gamma creating explosive potential in either direction once NFP hits. The gamma flip sits at $661 — well above current $642 levels — meaning any sustained rally faces immediate dealer selling pressure. Meanwhile, sector rotation accelerates with XLE's 86.7 technical score and overbought RSI contrasting sharply with oversold mega-cap tech (META, MSFT, GOOG all sub-30 RSI). Energy's strength shows in the XLE call blocks (11.2K Dec 62.5C, 7.5K Dec 47C) while tech remains under distribution. Cross-asset tensions emerge in the vol surface, where 9 names trade in backwardation (AAPL, AMD, AMZN, ARM, COIN, META) pricing elevated near-term risk, yet single-name vol remains structurally cheap to index vol. This divergence, combined with institutional positioning shifts — Millennium's massive AMZN reversal (+398% Q4) and Druckenmiller's full META exit — suggests smart money is rotating from high-multiple growth into cyclicals and energy ahead of potential Fed pivot signals from labor data.

Layer 1: Sentiment -55Layer 2: GEX -$13BLayer 3: P/C divergence 1.67/0.90Layer 9: NFP in 2d
